content:
From 2011 to 2026: Nostalgia, Unique Names, and School Memories 🎒🏫
Today, I found myself thinking about the year 2011 when I was just a Class 1 kid at St. Teresa School, Bhagalpur. I studied there from LKG to Class 1, before moving to DAV Public School, Kahalgaon, where I spent the next decade (2012 to 2022) from Class 2 to Class 10.

Even though it has been 14 years and we lost all contact back in 2012, I still clearly remember the names of my two best friends from St. Teresa. One of them actually lives quite close to me now, and I often wonder where life has taken them today. 🗺️✨

When I finally left DAV Public School in 2022, I was terrified that I would lose touch with my friends there too. Luckily, social media came to the rescue, and we are all still closely connected on Instagram. 📱❤️

Growing up through these schools, there was always one constant—my name. Sonabh Kinshuk is quite unique, so much so that most people struggle to pronounce it the first time! In fact, throughout my entire school life from 2012 to 2022, I never met another student with the same name. My friends always preferred calling me "Kinshuk" instead of Sonabh. 🫠

Now, as my CA journey has just started, I look back at my school days with a lot of pride. I was that disciplined student who was rarely absent. Unless it was a genuine health issue or an absolute emergency, I made it a point to show up to class every single day. 💯💪

That habit of showing up, which started in the classrooms of St. Teresa and DAV, is exactly what keeps me going today.

To old friends, unique names, and the discipline that shapes us! 🚀
